api_surface: Feeds API v2021-06-30 getFeed / processing report
drift_title: Feed DONE status can still contain item-level processing errors
silent_success_surface: getFeed can return processingStatus DONE for a submitted feed while the generated result document still carries per-message failures.
behavior_change: Catalog, pricing, inventory, or order-ack jobs that treat DONE as full success can miss rejected or partially applied feed records.
offline_blocker: Requires a live seller feed submission and the generated processing report document.
why_not_hard_break: The feed lifecycle completes normally; failures are encoded in the downstream result document rather than as request failure.

drift_title: Accepted listing updates can carry warning-level issues that change offer eligibility
silent_success_surface: Amazon documents VALID listing status with WARNING-level pricing issues where the listing update is accepted but downstream offer eligibility can differ.
behavior_change: Listing pipelines can record an accepted update while Featured Offer eligibility or pricing semantics drift because warnings are not hard errors.
offline_blocker: Requires a seller listing update that produces warning-level issues and live marketplace eligibility observation.
why_not_hard_break: The listing update is accepted; the behavior change is success-with-warning affecting downstream marketplace semantics.

drift_title: Reports can be automatically canceled when no eligible data is found
silent_success_surface: Reports API exposes CANCELLED as a terminal lifecycle status, including automatic cancellation when no data is available for the report.
behavior_change: Backfill jobs can get a valid terminal result with no report document and must distinguish no eligible data from failure.
offline_blocker: Requires live report requests across report types and date windows that produce no eligible report data.
why_not_hard_break: The report request lifecycle is valid and terminal; absence of data is represented as cancellation, not transport failure.

api_surface: Bulk Item Setup API / Feed status APIs
drift_title: Processed item feed can contain item-level submission errors
silent_success_surface: Walmart item setup returns a feedId and later PROCESSED means processing completed, while individual item submissions may carry errors.
behavior_change: Bulk catalog jobs can mark a feed as done while selected SKUs were not submitted or published.
offline_blocker: Requires a Walmart seller account and a mixed-validity item feed.
why_not_hard_break: The feed is accepted and processed; failures are per-item asynchronous outcomes.

api_surface: Bulk Inventory Update Feeds / Feed item status
drift_title: Bulk inventory feed acknowledgement only starts asynchronous line-level processing
silent_success_surface: Bulk inventory updates return a feed id, and sellers must inspect feed status and line-item status for each inventory update.
behavior_change: Inventory syncs can treat submission as success while specific SKUs or ship nodes remain unchanged.
offline_blocker: Requires live Walmart inventory feed submission and status polling.
why_not_hard_break: The API accepts the feed; finality is asynchronous and per-line.

drift_title: Fitment feed PROCESSED can mean processed with record errors
silent_success_surface: Walmart documents PROCESSED fitment feeds as either fully processed or processed with some records marked as errors downloadable through a separate API.
behavior_change: Fitment integrations can report a successful feed while selected compatibility records were rejected.
offline_blocker: Requires ACES or PIES fitment feed processing in a Walmart Marketplace seller context.
why_not_hard_break: The terminal feed status can be PROCESSED; rejected records are post-processing detail.

api_surface: REST asynchronous bulk endpoints / bulk operation status
drift_title: Bulk API accepts each request item before later per-operation failure
silent_success_surface: Adobe Commerce bulk endpoints split submitted arrays into queue messages and expose final per-operation status later.
behavior_change: Catalog or order integrations can record accepted bulk items as complete even though later operation statuses show retryable or non-retryable failures.
offline_blocker: Requires an Adobe Commerce instance with asynchronous queues and operations that fail during processing.
why_not_hard_break: The submit request succeeds and enqueues work; failure is asynchronous and per operation.

drift_title: ImportOperation can become partiallyImported when some actions succeed and others fail
silent_success_surface: commercetools documents partiallyImported as a state where some actions within an ImportOperation succeeded and others failed.
behavior_change: Bulk imports can mutate part of a product or inventory resource while leaving requested actions unapplied unless operation states are audited.
offline_blocker: Requires live Import API requests that trigger mixed action results.
why_not_hard_break: The import operation is a valid asynchronous resource state; partial mutation is represented in operation status.

drift_title: Order-driven inventory changes are eventually consistent for availability fields
silent_success_surface: Indirect inventory updates from order operations can be eventually consistent before availability fields reflect the new state.
behavior_change: Order placement can succeed while immediate inventory reads still show stale availableQuantity or quantityOnStock values.
offline_blocker: Requires a project using ReserveOnOrder or TrackOnly inventory modes and timed reads.
why_not_hard_break: The order operation and inventory reads are valid; delayed materialization is the documented consistency model.

api_surface: Rates API / Label Transaction API asynchronous responses
drift_title: Successful async rate or label request can omit the requested rate or label
silent_success_surface: Shippo default asynchronous rate and label calls can return a successful response before rates or label URLs exist on the resource.
behavior_change: Checkout or fulfillment systems can mark creation as successful before carrier data or label artifacts are materialized.
offline_blocker: Requires Shippo API calls using default asynchronous behavior against live carrier-backed rates or labels.
why_not_hard_break: The initial API call is successful; materialized carrier data arrives later.

drift_title: Manifest creation returns QUEUED before documents exist
silent_success_surface: Shippo manifest creation can return QUEUED while the manifest is generated and must be polled until SUCCESS or ERROR.
behavior_change: Warehouse closeout flows can treat manifest creation as complete before carrier acceptance documents are generated.
offline_blocker: Requires carrier-supported manifest creation through Shippo with live shipment transactions.
why_not_hard_break: The request creates a valid manifest resource; document generation is asynchronous.

drift_title: Batch shipment purchases are asynchronous with per-shipment failed statuses
silent_success_surface: EasyPost Batch operations expose aggregate batch state plus per-shipment statuses such as postage_purchase_failed and creation_failed.
behavior_change: Bulk label generation can progress or complete while individual shipments fail purchase or creation.
offline_blocker: Requires live EasyPost batch creation and batch buy operations with mixed-validity shipments.
why_not_hard_break: The batch resource and background job are valid; individual failures are represented inside batch state.

drift_title: Tracker data can remain unknown or delayed while the tracking resource is valid
silent_success_surface: EasyPost creates tracker resources that update in the background from carrier data; status can be unknown and location fields vary by carrier.
behavior_change: Shipment visibility systems can store a valid tracker while treating unknown or missing carrier fields as final.
offline_blocker: Requires live carrier tracking numbers and carrier-dependent update timing.
why_not_hard_break: The tracker API succeeds and continues updating; missing data reflects carrier availability and background polling.

drift_title: Processed label batch can finish as completed_with_errors
silent_success_surface: ShipEngine documents batch statuses including completed_with_errors and separate batch error listing for failed shipments.
behavior_change: Bulk shipping jobs can produce some labels while other shipments in the same batch fail.
offline_blocker: Requires live ShipEngine batch processing with mixed valid and invalid shipments.
why_not_hard_break: Batch processing is accepted; failures are batch-item outcomes.

drift_title: New trackings can stay Pending and later Expire without carrier updates
silent_success_surface: AfterShip newly added shipments can remain Pending while fetched in batches and later Expire if no carrier updates arrive.
behavior_change: Order-tracking systems can successfully create a tracking but see no carrier progress for hours or permanently.
offline_blocker: Requires live carrier tracking numbers and AfterShip account lifecycle observation.
why_not_hard_break: The tracking resource is valid; progress depends on delayed or absent carrier data.
notes: quality_gate=s4_or_strong_s3; direction=commerce; collected=2026-05-24; Strict audit boundary: AfterShip expired-status support URL is stale/unreachable; needs refreshed official source for the expiration claim.

drift_title: OCI inventory-affecting actions do not immediately change availability reads
silent_success_surface: Salesforce documents Omnichannel Inventory eventual consistency with asynchronous inventory data updates.
behavior_change: Checkout and order flows can succeed while immediate availability reads still show pre-order stock state.
offline_blocker: Requires Salesforce B2C Commerce with OCI enabled and timed order-versus-availability reads.
why_not_hard_break: Inventory-affecting actions and reads remain valid; drift is delayed materialization.
notes: quality_gate=s4_or_strong_s3; direction=commerce; collected=2026-05-24; Strict audit boundary: Salesforce B2C OCI source is not reachable in link checks; needs a stable official source or live proof.

drift_title: Hook return values can skip system implementation and later hooks
silent_success_surface: Hooks returning values for Shopper API extension points can skip the system implementation and subsequent registered hooks.
behavior_change: An API request can succeed while platform-default cart or response behavior is bypassed by custom hook return semantics.
offline_blocker: Requires a B2C Commerce instance with registered SCAPI or OCAPI hooks.
why_not_hard_break: The API request remains valid; custom extension semantics alter successful response processing.
notes: quality_gate=s4_or_strong_s3; direction=commerce; collected=2026-05-24; Strict audit boundary: Salesforce hook source no longer directly resolves to the claimed skip-system-implementation semantics; needs stable official reference.

api_surface: Conversions API / Meta Pixel event ingestion and matching
drift_title: Accepted Conversions API events are not guaranteed to become matched attributed events
silent_success_surface: Conversions API events can be sent and appear connected while final matching and attribution depend on match quality, user controls, and data-use restrictions.
behavior_change: Pipelines that treat event acceptance as campaign-attributed conversion truth can overstate usable optimization or attribution signal.
offline_blocker: Requires live Meta ad account, Pixel or CAPI traffic, match keys, and Ads Manager attribution surfaces.
why_not_hard_break: The event connection can be healthy; drift is downstream matching and policy-controlled use.

drift_title: Browser and server events can both be healthy while reporting counts only one deduplicated event
silent_success_surface: Pixel and CAPI paths can both submit the same business action successfully.
behavior_change: When deduplication keys align, Meta counts one event; when they do not, reports can undercount or double-count relative to the source system.
offline_blocker: Requires live Pixel plus CAPI dual instrumentation and reporting comparison.
why_not_hard_break: Both ingestion paths can accept events; drift is reconciliation semantics in reporting.

api_surface: Reddit Pixel and Conversions API custom conversion events
drift_title: Accepted custom conversion events outgrow dashboard visibility
silent_success_surface: Reddit supports custom conversion events, but the dashboard displays only the twenty most recent custom events.
behavior_change: Event uploads may continue while dashboard validation and reporting omit older custom event names.
offline_blocker: Requires live Reddit advertiser account with more than twenty custom conversion events.
why_not_hard_break: Events remain supported; the visibility cap affects reporting and validation surfaces.

drift_title: Dual Reddit Pixel and CAPI tracking requires deduplication to avoid reporting drift
silent_success_surface: Pixel and Conversions API can both capture the same conversion event.
behavior_change: Without deduplication, the same user action can be double-counted; with deduplication, one accepted path may not increase totals.
offline_blocker: Requires live Reddit Pixel plus CAPI implementation and reporting comparison.
why_not_hard_break: Both tracking methods are valid; event reconciliation changes reported conversion counts.

drift_title: X mobile conversion event can be recorded with no X attribution
silent_success_surface: POST /conversion_event records a mobile conversion event and can return a structured response with no X attribution.
behavior_change: Accepted event submission does not imply a campaign, line item, or promoted Tweet receives credit.
offline_blocker: Requires live X Ads app campaign engagements and hashed device identifiers.
why_not_hard_break: The API returns a normal conversion event response; attribution can legitimately be null.
notes: quality_gate=s4_or_strong_s3; direction=ads; collected=2026-05-24; strict_audit_downgraded_from_s4_no_live_proof=2026-05-24; Strict audit boundary: X Ads conversion-event source redirects away from the specific reference; needs current direct official source.

drift_title: X Ads async analytics job creation is not report readiness
silent_success_surface: POST stats/jobs/accounts returns a job_id for an accepted analytics request.
behavior_change: Metrics are unavailable until the job reaches SUCCESS and the compressed result file URL is ready.
offline_blocker: Requires live X Ads account and analytics jobs with measurable processing time.
why_not_hard_break: The reporting request is accepted; readiness is asynchronous and must be polled.
notes: quality_gate=s4_or_strong_s3; direction=ads; collected=2026-05-24; strict_audit_downgraded_from_s4_no_live_proof=2026-05-24; Strict audit boundary: X Ads async analytics sources redirect away from the specific reference; needs current direct official source.

api_surface: Reporting surfaces / Analytics reporting API data latency
drift_title: Adobe Analytics reports can be valid before collected data is complete
silent_success_surface: Reporting queries can return current data while Adobe is still processing batched collection data.
behavior_change: Recent-hour reports may understate traffic or conversions until processing latency clears.
offline_blocker: Requires live report suite traffic and reporting API queries during latency windows.
why_not_hard_break: Reports still return; completeness changes with backend processing latency.

api_surface: Reporting API dimensions with high cardinality
drift_title: Adobe Analytics high-cardinality dimensions collapse into Low-Traffic
silent_success_surface: Reports return successfully with a Low-Traffic dimension item after unique-value thresholds are exceeded.
behavior_change: Individual dimension values are bucketed, so reports silently lose row-level granularity.
offline_blocker: Requires live report suite dimension cardinality above Adobe thresholds.
why_not_hard_break: The report is valid; Adobe intentionally buckets excessive unique values.

drift_title: Amplitude attribution events can be held for 72 hours then dropped if no user match appears
silent_success_surface: Attribution API accepts campaign attribution events identified by advertising or vendor IDs.
behavior_change: If Amplitude cannot match the attribution event to an existing user within 72 hours, the attribution data is dropped.
offline_blocker: Requires live attribution requests for identifiers that may or may not later match ingested users.
why_not_hard_break: Upload can be accepted; later matching determines whether attribution data survives.

drift_title: Amplitude Export API can return no data before export lag clears
silent_success_surface: Export API calls for recent upload-time ranges can return normal behavior before data is available to export.
behavior_change: Recent ETLs can miss events unless delayed or replayed after export availability catches up.
offline_blocker: Requires live Amplitude project with recent event ingestion and export polling.
why_not_hard_break: The API behaves as documented; export availability lags ingestion.

api_surface: Experiment targeting with cohorts / remote evaluation
drift_title: Amplitude Experiment cohort targeting can lag variant eligibility
silent_success_surface: Experiment evaluation requests continue to return variants or no variant while cohort and CDN state update hourly.
behavior_change: A user may be delayed from becoming targetable for up to two hours after relevant property or cohort changes.
offline_blocker: Requires live experiment with dynamic cohort targeting and timed user property changes.
why_not_hard_break: Evaluation still succeeds; eligibility state is stale by design.

drift_title: Mixpanel track 200 can mean at least one valid object, not full batch validity
silent_success_surface: Track endpoint can return HTTP 200 with success semantics for one or more valid objects.
behavior_change: Batch senders can treat a 200 as full ingestion even when some objects were invalid or require verbose inspection.
offline_blocker: Requires live Mixpanel project and mixed-validity batched tracking payloads.
why_not_hard_break: The request returns 200; completeness depends on object-level validity.

drift_title: Mixpanel ingested duplicate events can disappear from reports after query-time or compaction dedup
silent_success_surface: Import API can return 200 for ingested records while deduplication changes report-visible counts.
behavior_change: Events sharing dedupe keys may be hidden from reports and later compacted even if raw exports still contain duplicates.
offline_blocker: Requires live project with duplicate insert IDs and report/export comparison.
why_not_hard_break: Ingestion succeeds; later reporting and storage deduplication change visible counts.

api_surface: SKAN aggregated performance report API / SKAN modeled data
drift_title: AppsFlyer SKAN reports mix delayed Apple postbacks with modeled hidden data
silent_success_surface: SKAN report APIs return aggregate performance data from delayed Apple postbacks and modeled hidden data.
behavior_change: Reports can be valid while conversion values are hidden, delayed, or modeled rather than directly observed.
offline_blocker: Requires live iOS SKAN campaigns and AppsFlyer SKAN reporting access.
why_not_hard_break: Reports return normally; data is delayed, privacy-limited, and modeled by design.
notes: quality_gate=s4_or_strong_s3; direction=ads; collected=2026-05-24; strict_audit_downgraded_from_s4_no_live_proof=2026-05-24; Strict audit boundary: AppsFlyer support sources are blocked/unreachable in link checks; needs reachable official evidence or live proof.

api_surface: SKAN raw data reports / Data Locker / Push API
drift_title: AppsFlyer SKAN raw rows contain privacy-threshold-dependent missing fields
silent_success_surface: SKAN raw data reports can be delivered while Apple privacy thresholds suppress specific values.
behavior_change: Row-level exports can be structurally present but analytically incomplete.
offline_blocker: Requires live SKAN postbacks across privacy tiers and raw report access.
why_not_hard_break: Rows and files are delivered; privacy thresholds suppress fields.
notes: quality_gate=s4_or_strong_s3; direction=ads; collected=2026-05-24; strict_audit_downgraded_from_s4_no_live_proof=2026-05-24; Strict audit boundary: AppsFlyer SKAN raw-data/privacy-threshold sources are blocked/unreachable in link checks; needs reachable official evidence or live proof.

drift_title: Adjust attribution can fall back from deterministic matching to probabilistic modeling
silent_success_surface: Attribution reporting continues to produce attributed installs when deterministic identifiers are unavailable.
behavior_change: The attribution method and confidence can change under privacy or identifier availability differences.
offline_blocker: Requires live Adjust app traffic under identifier availability differences and partner engagement logs.
why_not_hard_break: Attribution still reports; the method and certainty change.

drift_title: Branch Daily Exports API can return an empty array before daily job completion
silent_success_surface: Daily Exports API can succeed for a date whose data file has not finished processing.
behavior_change: ETLs scheduled too early can treat an empty array as no events even though the file appears later.
offline_blocker: Requires live Branch app data and polling before and after the daily export job finishes.
why_not_hard_break: The API response is valid; file availability is delayed by batch processing.

drift_title: DynamoDB GSI reads can lag successful table writes
silent_success_surface: A table write can return success while a read from a global secondary index still reflects an earlier index state.
behavior_change: Systems that validate write success through a GSI can observe stale or missing items after a successful write.
offline_blocker: Requires live DynamoDB table writes, a GSI, and timed post-write reads.
why_not_hard_break: The write and query APIs return normally; the drift is documented read consistency.

drift_title: Kinesis PutRecord timeout can produce duplicate records
silent_success_surface: A PutRecord call can time out on the client even though the record was committed and a retry writes another record.
behavior_change: Downstream processors can receive duplicate business events after apparently retried failed writes.
offline_blocker: Requires live Kinesis writes under timeout or retry conditions.
why_not_hard_break: The service may accept both writes; duplicate delivery is an online retry semantic, not endpoint deletion.

drift_title: EventBridge target delivery can exhaust retries and drop or send to DLQ
silent_success_surface: Events can match a rule and be accepted for delivery while target invocation fails until retry policy is exhausted.
behavior_change: Producers see event publication success while selected targets never apply the event unless DLQ or metrics are inspected.
offline_blocker: Requires live EventBridge rules, targets, and failure or throttling conditions.
why_not_hard_break: Event publication and rule matching can succeed; downstream delivery is asynchronous and retry-bounded.

drift_title: SNS HTTP delivery can retry and eventually discard while publish succeeds
silent_success_surface: SNS Publish can succeed while HTTP endpoint delivery retries according to policy and can later fail permanently.
behavior_change: Webhook consumers can miss notifications even though the topic accepted the message.
offline_blocker: Requires live SNS topic subscription and endpoint behavior causing delivery retries or failure.
why_not_hard_break: Publish success is separate from endpoint delivery finality.

drift_title: Async Express workflows provide at-least-once execution semantics
silent_success_surface: Starting an Async Express workflow can succeed while steps may be retried or executed more than once.
behavior_change: Side-effecting integrations can observe duplicate downstream operations from one accepted workflow start.
offline_blocker: Requires live Express workflow execution with side-effecting tasks and retries.
why_not_hard_break: The workflow start succeeds; at-least-once execution is documented service behavior.

api_surface: Delivery stream destination retries and backup
drift_title: Firehose can accept records before later destination skip or backup behavior
silent_success_surface: PutRecordBatch can accept records while destination delivery later retries, fails, or sends records to backup/error locations.
behavior_change: Ingestion systems can count accepted records as delivered even when final destination materialization differs.
offline_blocker: Requires live Firehose stream, destination configuration, and destination failure or throttling.
why_not_hard_break: Record ingestion and destination delivery are separate phases.

drift_title: Azure Service Bus duplicate send can be accepted then dropped by duplicate detection
silent_success_surface: A sender can receive success-like behavior for a duplicate message ID while the broker discards the duplicate within the detection window.
behavior_change: Producers can believe a retry enqueued a new message when the broker intentionally suppressed it.
offline_blocker: Requires live Service Bus entity with duplicate detection enabled and duplicate message IDs.
why_not_hard_break: The send operation is valid; duplicate suppression is broker semantics.

drift_title: Azure Queue messages can be redelivered and ordering is best effort
silent_success_surface: A dequeue can succeed while failure to delete before visibility timeout makes the same message visible again.
behavior_change: Workers can process duplicate messages or observe non-FIFO order after apparently normal queue operations.
offline_blocker: Requires live Azure Queue processing with visibility timeouts and competing consumers.
why_not_hard_break: Queue operations work; redelivery is the documented reliability model.

drift_title: Event Hubs trigger checkpoint replay can duplicate successful processing
silent_success_surface: A function invocation can process events successfully before checkpoint persistence, so failure or scale movement can replay them.
behavior_change: Consumers can observe duplicate side effects after accepted Event Hubs delivery.
offline_blocker: Requires live Event Hubs trigger processing and checkpoint or failure timing.
why_not_hard_break: Delivery and processing can succeed; checkpoint boundaries govern replay.

drift_title: Cosmos DB reads without the session token can behave as eventual after successful writes
silent_success_surface: Writes can succeed under session consistency while a later client lacking the session token reads older data.
behavior_change: Distributed clients can see stale reads after successful writes when session tokens are not propagated.
offline_blocker: Requires live Cosmos DB account under session consistency and multi-client token handling.
why_not_hard_break: Reads and writes return normally; semantics depend on documented session-token propagation.

drift_title: List Blobs can return a partial page with NextMarker
silent_success_surface: A list request can return a valid page and NextMarker rather than all blobs.
behavior_change: Scanners that treat the first 2xx list result as complete silently omit later pages.
offline_blocker: Requires a live container with enough blobs or prefix filtering to page results.
why_not_hard_break: The request succeeds; pagination is the completeness boundary.

drift_title: Cloud Tasks can dispatch duplicate tasks and does not guarantee ordering
silent_success_surface: Task creation and dispatch APIs can succeed while delivery is at-least-once and order is not guaranteed.
behavior_change: Workers can see duplicate or reordered side effects after apparently healthy task queue operation.
offline_blocker: Requires live Cloud Tasks queue and worker callbacks under retry or load timing.
why_not_hard_break: Task delivery is working; at-least-once and ordering semantics are documented.

drift_title: Eventarc can deliver duplicate CloudEvents
silent_success_surface: Eventarc delivery can retry and duplicate events even when the event trigger is correctly configured.
behavior_change: Consumers must dedupe by event ID or source semantics to avoid duplicate downstream effects.
offline_blocker: Requires live Eventarc trigger and receiver behavior that causes retry or duplicate delivery.
why_not_hard_break: Triggering succeeds; duplicate delivery is a documented reliability semantic.

drift_title: Cloud Run functions can drop events when retry is disabled even though event flow appears healthy
silent_success_surface: A background event can be delivered to a function and fail; if retries are disabled, the event is not attempted again.
behavior_change: Pipelines can lose side effects without a hard producer error unless failures and retry settings are monitored.
offline_blocker: Requires live event-driven Cloud Run functions and failure scenarios.
why_not_hard_break: Event delivery and invocation can happen; retry policy determines later recovery.

drift_title: BigQuery streaming inserts can be accepted before all downstream surfaces see the rows
silent_success_surface: Streaming inserts can be accepted while data remains in the streaming buffer and is unavailable to some copy/export or metadata surfaces.
behavior_change: ETLs can treat insert success as immediate full warehouse materialization and miss buffered rows.
offline_blocker: Requires live BigQuery streaming inserts and immediate copy/export/metadata reads.
why_not_hard_break: Insert acceptance is not the same as availability across every BigQuery surface.

drift_title: Cloudflare Queues can deliver duplicate messages
silent_success_surface: Queues provide at-least-once delivery, so a produced message can be delivered more than once.
behavior_change: Consumers can observe duplicate side effects after successful message production.
offline_blocker: Requires live Cloudflare Queue consumer processing and retry/failure timing.
why_not_hard_break: Queue publish and delivery work; duplicate delivery is part of the guarantee model.

drift_title: Workers KV reads can be stale after successful writes
silent_success_surface: Workers KV writes can succeed while globally replicated reads or negative-cache entries remain stale for a period.
behavior_change: Edge consumers can see missing or old values after a successful update.
offline_blocker: Requires live Workers KV writes and reads from different edge locations or cached keys.
why_not_hard_break: The write and read APIs succeed; global eventual consistency is documented.

drift_title: Braintree settlement_pending can later become declined
silent_success_surface: A transaction can reach settlement_pending before processor settlement later declines it.
behavior_change: Order systems that ship on settlement_pending can miss later settlement failure.
offline_blocker: Requires live Braintree transactions and processor settlement lifecycle observation.
why_not_hard_break: The transaction lifecycle is valid; final settlement can change after accepted processing.

drift_title: Confirmed Direct Debit payments can later fail late
silent_success_surface: A Direct Debit payment can appear confirmed before late failure is reported through payment status and events.
behavior_change: Merchants can provision service after confirmation and later need reversal or dunning flows.
offline_blocker: Requires live Direct Debit mandates and banking return windows.
why_not_hard_break: Payment creation and confirmation are valid lifecycle states; late failure is a downstream banking outcome.

drift_title: Instalment Schedule pending can later produce creation_failed or payment_errors
silent_success_surface: An instalment schedule can be created and remain pending while future payment creation or collection errors appear later.
behavior_change: Subscription systems can treat schedule creation as guaranteed future cash collection.
offline_blocker: Requires live GoCardless instalment schedule execution across collection dates.
why_not_hard_break: The schedule resource is valid; individual generated payments can fail later.

drift_title: Executed payment is not the same as settled funds
silent_success_surface: A payment can reach executed while settlement and reconciliation outcomes remain separate.
behavior_change: Treasury systems that treat executed as final settled cash can overstate available funds.
offline_blocker: Requires live TrueLayer payments and settlement or reconciliation observation.
why_not_hard_break: The payment API succeeds; executed is an intermediate lifecycle meaning.

drift_title: Executed payout can later be returned or fail
silent_success_surface: A payout can be accepted or executed before downstream banking return states arrive.
behavior_change: Disbursement systems can mark a payout as complete before recipient funds are final.
offline_blocker: Requires live TrueLayer payouts and downstream banking return conditions.
why_not_hard_break: The payout lifecycle is valid; finality depends on later banking events.

drift_title: Flutterwave transfer creation status must be verified after NEW
silent_success_surface: Transfer creation can return an accepted or NEW state while final success or failure is determined by verification.
behavior_change: Payout jobs can treat transfer submission as settled credit and miss later failure.
offline_blocker: Requires live Flutterwave transfer creation and verification polling.
why_not_hard_break: The transfer is accepted for processing; final status is asynchronous.

drift_title: Flutterwave refund pending can later fail
silent_success_surface: A refund can be created and remain pending before a later failed or successful result is available.
behavior_change: Refund workflows can notify customers before funds are actually returned.
offline_blocker: Requires live Flutterwave refund requests and delayed processor outcome.
why_not_hard_break: The refund request succeeds; refund settlement is asynchronous.

drift_title: Paystack transfer success is not necessarily instant recipient credit
silent_success_surface: Paystack transfers can report success while bank credit timing or reversal behavior differs.
behavior_change: Disbursement systems can mark payouts fully delivered before recipient bank posting or reversal is final.
offline_blocker: Requires live Paystack transfers and bank-side posting or reversal observation.
why_not_hard_break: The transfer API can succeed; bank credit finality is downstream.
notes: quality_gate=s4_or_strong_s3; direction=payments; collected=2026-05-24; strict_audit_downgraded_from_s4_no_live_proof=2026-05-24; Strict audit boundary: Paystack transfer sources are blocked/unreachable in link checks; needs reachable official evidence or live proof.

drift_title: Paystack refund statuses can remain pending or need attention before customer funds arrive
silent_success_surface: Refund APIs expose pending, processed, and needs-attention style lifecycle states rather than immediate final return of funds.
behavior_change: Refund jobs can count a refund request as complete before the customer receives funds.
offline_blocker: Requires live Paystack refund requests across processor settlement timing.
why_not_hard_break: The refund request is valid; completion and customer funds are asynchronous.
notes: quality_gate=s4_or_strong_s3; direction=payments; collected=2026-05-24; strict_audit_downgraded_from_s4_no_live_proof=2026-05-24; Strict audit boundary: Paystack refund source is blocked/unreachable in link checks; needs reachable official evidence or live proof.

drift_title: Dwolla processed ACH transfers can later return
silent_success_surface: An ACH transfer can reach processed before an ACH return is received within the banking return window.
behavior_change: Ledger systems can make funds available after processed and later need reversal handling.
offline_blocker: Requires live ACH transfers and return-window observation.
why_not_hard_break: The transfer lifecycle is valid; ACH returns are downstream banking events.

drift_title: Column ACH transfer can settle before the completed return window closes
silent_success_surface: ACH states can indicate settlement while the return window and final completion state are distinct.
behavior_change: Treasury systems can treat settlement as irrevocable and miss later return risk.
offline_blocker: Requires live Column ACH transfers and return-window observation.
why_not_hard_break: The transfer state is valid; banking finality is time-window dependent.

drift_title: Column international wire completed can later be returned with FX difference
silent_success_surface: International wires can reach completed before a later returned status and returned amount differences are known.
behavior_change: Payout systems can mark beneficiary delivery final and later need reconciliation with FX-adjusted return values.
offline_blocker: Requires live international wire transfer lifecycle and return events.
why_not_hard_break: The wire state is valid; cross-border banking can return funds later.

drift_title: Originated ACH sent can later be returned
silent_success_surface: Originating ACH files can be sent while later ACH return events change the outcome.
behavior_change: Banking apps can provision funds or service after sent and later need to reverse.
offline_blocker: Requires live Unit ACH origination and return-window observation.
why_not_hard_break: The ACH send is valid; return risk is downstream network behavior.

drift_title: Received ACH can move through pending, advanced, completed, and returned states
silent_success_surface: Incoming ACH can be advanced or completed before later return semantics are visible.
behavior_change: Deposit systems can show available funds while return handling still matters.
offline_blocker: Requires live received ACH events and return-window observation.
why_not_hard_break: The received ACH states are valid; finality changes through documented lifecycle states.

drift_title: Plaid Transactions Sync can modify or remove previously returned transactions
silent_success_surface: Transactions Sync returns added, modified, and removed entries rather than an immutable append-only feed.
behavior_change: Finance apps can treat accepted prior transactions as stable and miss later corrections or removals.
offline_blocker: Requires live Plaid Items and account data whose institution feed changes over time.
why_not_hard_break: Sync succeeds; the drift is a documented mutable financial data model.

drift_title: Teller pending transactions can shift date or receive a new id when posted
silent_success_surface: Pending transactions can later post with changed details rather than preserving an immutable pending object.
behavior_change: Expense systems can double-count or lose reconciliation if pending and posted items are assumed identical.
offline_blocker: Requires live bank account transactions moving from pending to posted.
why_not_hard_break: The API returns valid transactions; the bank feed changes across settlement lifecycle.

drift_title: Straddle paid payment can later be reversed
silent_success_surface: Payment lifecycle statuses can move from paid to a reversed outcome due to later network or bank events.
behavior_change: Applications can mark access final on paid and later need clawback handling.
offline_blocker: Requires live Straddle payment lifecycle and reversal events.
why_not_hard_break: The paid status is valid; finality can change through later payment status transitions.

drift_title: Okta UserCredentials password can be an empty object due to redaction
silent_success_surface: A user retrieval can return a credentials password object without secret material.
behavior_change: Auditors or sync jobs can mistake a redacted object for absent or unusable credentials.
offline_blocker: Requires live Okta tenant user credentials and privileged read scopes.
why_not_hard_break: The API response is valid; secret material is intentionally redacted.

drift_title: Okta resetPasswordUrl behavior depends on sendEmail semantics
silent_success_surface: A password reset operation can return a reset URL or send an email depending on request parameters.
behavior_change: Automation can treat a successful reset call as delivering an email or producing a URL when the opposite occurred.
offline_blocker: Requires live Okta users and reset password invocation.
why_not_hard_break: The operation succeeds; output semantics depend on documented parameters.

drift_title: Okta okta-response can omit credentials or transition fields
silent_success_surface: Okta response hints can suppress credentials or transition fields in otherwise successful responses.
behavior_change: Provisioning consumers can treat omitted fields as absent state rather than deliberate response shaping.
offline_blocker: Requires live Okta tenant calls using response hints or create-user flows.
why_not_hard_break: The API succeeds; field visibility changes through documented response controls.

drift_title: Auth0 userinfo email/profile fields are scope gated
silent_success_surface: userinfo calls can succeed while email, profile, or other claims are omitted because scopes were not granted.
behavior_change: Identity syncs can overwrite or miss attributes after treating successful userinfo as complete.
offline_blocker: Requires live Auth0 application, users, and token scope variations.
why_not_hard_break: The endpoint succeeds; claim inclusion is scope-dependent.

drift_title: Auth0 tokens can have fewer granted scopes than requested
silent_success_surface: Authorization can complete while the access token contains only granted scopes.
behavior_change: Clients can assume requested permissions exist and silently lose downstream field or action access.
offline_blocker: Requires live Auth0 tenant configuration and consent or API scope policy.
why_not_hard_break: The auth flow succeeds; granted scopes are the contract, not requested scopes.

drift_title: Google OAuth refresh_token is usually returned only on first authorization
silent_success_surface: OAuth exchange can succeed without a refresh_token on subsequent grants.
behavior_change: Token stores can overwrite an existing refresh token with null after a successful exchange.
offline_blocker: Requires live Google OAuth consent history and repeated authorization exchanges.
why_not_hard_break: The token response is successful; refresh token issuance is conditional.

drift_title: Google OIDC optional claims can be withheld despite successful authentication
silent_success_surface: ID token or userinfo responses can succeed while optional profile claims are absent.
behavior_change: Profile syncs can treat omitted claims as deleted user data.
offline_blocker: Requires live Google accounts with varied profile visibility and scopes.
why_not_hard_break: Authentication succeeds; claim emission depends on scopes and profile data.

drift_title: Admin SDK users.get can return domain_public field subset
silent_success_surface: A users.get call can succeed under domain_public view while returning only limited public fields.
behavior_change: Directory syncs can interpret missing private fields as absent or cleared attributes.
offline_blocker: Requires live Workspace tenant and requests using alternate viewType or privilege context.
why_not_hard_break: The response is valid; field completeness is controlled by view type and authorization.

drift_title: Admin SDK custom schemas are omitted unless requested by projection
silent_success_surface: Users list or get calls can succeed while custom schema fields are absent under default projection.
behavior_change: Provisioning jobs can erase or miss custom attributes when treating default projections as complete.
offline_blocker: Requires live Workspace custom schemas and projection variations.
why_not_hard_break: The request succeeds; custom field inclusion is projection-dependent.

drift_title: Google Admin user update can ignore read-only fields while applying writable fields
silent_success_surface: Update requests can succeed for writable fields while read-only fields are not changed.
behavior_change: Clients can treat a successful update as full object convergence when selected fields were ignored.
offline_blocker: Requires live Workspace user updates containing read-only and writable fields.
why_not_hard_break: The update is valid for mutable fields; read-only fields remain controlled by the service.

drift_title: AWS STS GetCallerIdentity returns identity even under explicit deny
silent_success_surface: GetCallerIdentity can return the caller identity even when an explicit deny is attached.
behavior_change: Auth probes can misclassify denied credentials as authorized because identity lookup succeeds.
offline_blocker: Requires live AWS credentials with deny policy attached.
why_not_hard_break: The API is documented to return identity; authorization for other actions is a separate contract.

drift_title: AccessKeyLastUsed can be null or N/A for tracked usage gaps
silent_success_surface: IAM access key last-used data can be unavailable for services, regions, or never-used keys.
behavior_change: Security cleanup jobs can treat missing last-used data as evidence of no usage.
offline_blocker: Requires live AWS access keys and historical service usage patterns.
why_not_hard_break: The API succeeds; last-used telemetry has documented coverage limitations.

drift_title: BatchGetSecretValue can return HTTP success with per-secret errors
silent_success_surface: A batch request can return a 200 response with Values for some secrets and Errors for others.
behavior_change: Secret fetchers can treat the batch as complete and silently omit failed secrets.
offline_blocker: Requires live AWS account with a mixed set of permitted, missing, or failing secret fetches.
why_not_hard_break: The batch API succeeds; failures are item-level response members.

drift_title: Cognito GetUser attributes are gated by readable-attribute app client settings
silent_success_surface: GetUser can succeed while attributes not marked readable for the app client are omitted.
behavior_change: Identity profiles can lose fields when sync jobs assume successful GetUser is complete.
offline_blocker: Requires live Cognito user pool app clients with varied readable attribute settings.
why_not_hard_break: The API call succeeds; attribute visibility is configured per app client.

drift_title: Cognito ID token can omit non-readable user attributes
silent_success_surface: Authentication can succeed while ID token claims exclude attributes not readable by the app client.
behavior_change: Clients can treat missing token claims as missing user data rather than visibility policy.
offline_blocker: Requires live Cognito app client and token issuance with varied readable attributes.
why_not_hard_break: Auth succeeds; token claim inclusion is governed by app client attribute permissions.

drift_title: GuardDuty suppressed findings are archived and not sent through normal downstream flows
silent_success_surface: Findings can match suppression rules and be archived rather than appearing in standard active finding workflows.
behavior_change: Security pipelines can miss real findings if they only consume unsuppressed active streams.
offline_blocker: Requires live GuardDuty detector findings and suppression rules.
why_not_hard_break: GuardDuty operates normally; suppression changes visibility and delivery semantics.

drift_title: Salesforce Bulk API 2.0 accepted jobs can later have row failures
silent_success_surface: A Bulk API ingest job can be uploaded and processed while failed-results endpoints contain per-row errors.
behavior_change: Data loaders can mark a job as successful while selected records were rejected.
offline_blocker: Requires live Salesforce org, Bulk API ingest job, and mixed-validity records.
why_not_hard_break: The job lifecycle succeeds; failures are row-level asynchronous outcomes.

drift_title: Salesforce Composite can return outer success with subrequest errors
silent_success_surface: Composite API calls can return a successful outer response while individual subresponses carry errors.
behavior_change: Integrations can treat the envelope status as complete and miss failed records or partial effects.
offline_blocker: Requires live Salesforce org and composite calls with mixed-validity subrequests.
why_not_hard_break: The composite request is accepted; subrequest status is the actual operation boundary.

drift_title: Zoho Bulk Write accepted job can later contain record failures
silent_success_surface: Bulk Write creates an asynchronous job and later result files report record-level success or failure.
behavior_change: CRM imports can mark a job accepted while selected records fail validation or lookup.
offline_blocker: Requires live Zoho CRM org and bulk write job with mixed-validity records.
why_not_hard_break: The job is valid and asynchronous; row outcomes are reported later.

drift_title: Zoho Upsert duplicate_check can update existing records instead of inserting
silent_success_surface: An upsert request can succeed while duplicate_check rules cause existing records to be updated.
behavior_change: Lead capture pipelines can count accepted upserts as new records when they actually mutated existing ones.
offline_blocker: Requires live Zoho CRM duplicate fields and records matching duplicate_check.
why_not_hard_break: The request succeeds; insert-versus-update semantics depend on duplicate matching.

drift_title: Microsoft Graph sendMail 202 is not delivery completion
silent_success_surface: sendMail returns 202 Accepted and does not mean the message has been delivered.
behavior_change: Notification systems can mark email delivered while Exchange transport can later fail or delay it.
offline_blocker: Requires live Microsoft 365 mailbox and mail transport observation.
why_not_hard_break: The API accepts the send request; delivery is asynchronous mail transport.

drift_title: Microsoft Graph JSON batch can have outer success with inner failures
silent_success_surface: A JSON batch envelope can be accepted while individual requests in the batch fail.
behavior_change: Sync jobs can treat one batch response as full success and silently skip failed operations.
offline_blocker: Requires live Graph calls with mixed valid and invalid subrequests.
why_not_hard_break: The batch envelope succeeds; each subresponse is independently authoritative.

drift_title: Gmail watch notifications can be rate-limited or dropped while mailbox changes continue
silent_success_surface: Gmail push notifications are a live signal and clients must use history sync to recover from dropped or rate-limited notifications.
behavior_change: Email processors can miss changes if they treat delivered notifications as a complete changelog.
offline_blocker: Requires live Gmail mailbox, watch channel, and notification/history timing.
why_not_hard_break: Mailbox changes and watch setup succeed; notification delivery is not a complete durable log.

drift_title: WhatsApp Cloud API accepted message can later fail delivery
silent_success_surface: Message send calls can return accepted message IDs while later status webhooks report failed delivery.
behavior_change: Messaging flows can mark communication complete before final delivery status.
offline_blocker: Requires live WhatsApp Business account, recipient conditions, and webhook capture.
why_not_hard_break: The send request succeeds; delivery status is asynchronous.

drift_title: Vonage submitted messages can later become rejected or undeliverable
silent_success_surface: A Messages API send can be accepted into the messaging pipeline while status callbacks later report rejection or failure.
behavior_change: Customer communication systems can count submissions as delivered messages.
offline_blocker: Requires live Vonage application, provider routing, and status callback capture.
why_not_hard_break: The send is accepted; carrier or channel delivery is asynchronous.

drift_title: Sinch Conversation messages can fail or switch channel after accepted send
silent_success_surface: Conversation API can accept a message while callbacks later report delivery failure or channel-specific processing changes.
behavior_change: Omnichannel systems can assume the requested channel delivered when final callbacks show a different outcome.
offline_blocker: Requires live Sinch app, channel routing, and callback capture.
why_not_hard_break: The send request works; final delivery and channel result are callback-driven.

drift_title: Telnyx accepted messages can later reach delivery_failed
silent_success_surface: Message creation can be accepted while later delivery status transitions report failure.
behavior_change: SMS systems can mark an accepted message as delivered before carrier outcome arrives.
offline_blocker: Requires live Telnyx messaging profile, carrier route, and webhook status updates.
why_not_hard_break: The API accepts the message; final delivery is asynchronous carrier state.

drift_title: Plivo queued or sent messages can later fail or become undelivered
silent_success_surface: Message API responses and callbacks expose queued or sent states before terminal failed or undelivered states.
behavior_change: Notification systems can overcount delivered SMS when they only inspect submission state.
offline_blocker: Requires live Plivo messaging and status callback observation.
why_not_hard_break: Message submission succeeds; carrier delivery state changes later.

drift_title: Brevo transactional messageId can later bounce, block, or error
silent_success_surface: Transactional send can return a messageId while later webhook events report bounce, blocked, deferred, or error outcomes.
behavior_change: Email workflows can mark an email delivered from send acceptance alone.
offline_blocker: Requires live Brevo transactional email sending and webhook event capture.
why_not_hard_break: The send request is accepted; deliverability outcome is asynchronous.

drift_title: MailerSend queued messages can later soft-bounce or hard-bounce
silent_success_surface: MailerSend exposes delivery states where a queued message can later bounce.
behavior_change: Transactional systems can count queued email as final delivery.
offline_blocker: Requires live MailerSend account, email sends, and webhook status observation.
why_not_hard_break: The email send is accepted; final delivery is an asynchronous mail transport result.

drift_title: Zendesk create_many returns an asynchronous job before per-ticket results
silent_success_surface: Bulk ticket creation can return a job_status URL while final ticket creation results are computed later.
behavior_change: Support migrations can treat accepted bulk creation as complete before failed tickets are known.
offline_blocker: Requires live Zendesk subdomain and create_many jobs with mixed-validity tickets.
why_not_hard_break: The request starts a valid job; per-ticket outcomes are asynchronous.

drift_title: Help Scout webhooks can retry and duplicate event delivery
silent_success_surface: Webhook subscriptions can deliver the same event more than once due to retry behavior.
behavior_change: Downstream CRM automations can apply duplicate updates after apparently healthy webhook delivery.
offline_blocker: Requires live Help Scout mailbox webhooks and retry-triggering endpoint behavior.
why_not_hard_break: Webhook delivery is working; duplicate delivery is a reliability and retry semantic.
